steps.title: Recognize this requires a weighted mean
steps.content:
We might initially think to just average the two group means: (87 + 93) / 2 = 90. But this ignores that the two groups have different sizes. The larger group (380 from Kerguelen) should influence the overall mean more than the smaller group (190 from Falkland). We need a weighted mean where each group's contribution is proportional to its size:

$$\text{Overall Mean} = \frac{(\text{size}_1 \times \text{mean}_1) + (\text{size}_2 \times \text{mean}_2)}{\text{total size}}$$

steps.title: Calculate the weighted mean
steps.content:
Apply the formula:

$$\text{Kerguelen contribution} = 380 \times 87 = 33{,}060 \text{ cm}$$

$$\text{Falkland contribution} = 190 \times 93 = 17{,}670 \text{ cm}$$

$$\text{Overall Mean} = \frac{33{,}060 + 17{,}670}{380 + 190} = \frac{50{,}730}{570} = 89$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
